note: LS-1..LS-7 conceptual fixtures (Decision 0015). Deterministic consistency artifacts: they exercise the six-way typed distinction (orthemma / analysis / optional latent state / observation / latent candidate set or belief / inferred partial profile / concrete internal representation) and the anti-conflation rules. The latent layer is OPTIONAL and NON-PRIMITIVE. No empirical claim; nothing here validates or is validated by any cited literature.
conflation_note: Every fixture must declare ALL ten keys and every one of them must be false. The validator fails closed on any true value: these are the identities Decision 0015 forbids, not configurable options.

fixtures.title: aliased observation with consequentially distinct states
fixtures.setup: Four sensorily identical regions share one observation under a declared non-injective observation map. The task requires different action in two of them, so merging the corresponding latent states would alter a warranted prediction and a route beyond the declared tolerance. No sequence or context evidence has arrived yet at time t.

typed_objects.orthemma: m_t: this time-indexed encounter with one of the four identical regions
typed_objects.observation: x_t = Omega_A(m_t): the currently visible ambiguous region; Omega_A is non-injective here
typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t = {z_A, z_B, z_C, z_D} subset of Z_A, model-internal labels only
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): mass spread across all four candidates, below the bound threshold
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_t: the current activity/embedding vector, geometry not yet separated
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m) in Pi_A^partial: region-identity component left unfilled

metaorthemma.reason: non-default history window, bound inference threshold, instrument provenance, independent validity interval

fixtures.title: safe latent-state merge (a model-state difference forces no new ortheme)
fixtures.setup: The fitted model carries two distinct latent states for what the declared analysis treats as one case kind. Merging them changes no warranted prediction, classification, investigation, route, validation step, closure condition or evaluation beyond the declared tolerance, and violates no hard constraint. The admission test of Decision 0015 section 2 fails.

typed_objects.orthemma: m_t: this encounter, unremarkable under A
typed_objects.observation: x_t = Omega_A(m_t): fully determining at the governed level
typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t = {z_7, z_12}: two model-internal states, both consistent
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): split across z_7 and z_12, and the split is orthemically inert
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_t: two mildly separated clusters in representation space
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m): complete at the governed level despite the latent split

metaorthemma.reason: zero-burden rule: no material case-specific binding, default scope, no independent validity condition; GOV_TOKEN_ADEQUATE recorded not-applicable

fixtures.title: high performance without global orthogonalization
fixtures.setup: A system meets every declared task requirement while its internal representation is not globally orthogonalized; the separation it needs lives in a low-dimensional task-relevant readout subspace. No verdict fails on the ground of representational geometry alone. Representational geometry and pathway adequacy are separate questions.

typed_objects.observation: x_t = Omega_A(m_t): the input actually received
typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t: singleton after context, adequacy independent of geometry
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): concentrated, above the bound threshold
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_t: globally entangled, separable only within the readout subspace
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m): correct at the governed level and tolerance

fixtures.title: same endpoint, different trajectory
fixtures.setup: Two processes reach an identical final representational configuration by materially different intermediate sequences. Endpoint equivalence does not imply pathway or mechanism equivalence; the trajectory carries discriminating evidence that endpoint comparison lacks. This is the result/pathway separation of Decision 0003 applied to the latent layer.

typed_objects.orthemma: m_t: this run, considered as a case; the reified episode iota_n(e)
typed_objects.observation: x_t = Omega_A(m_t): the endpoint measurement actually taken
typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t: the mechanisms still consistent with the endpoint, plural
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): endpoint evidence does not concentrate it
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_T: matching terminal geometry in both processes
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m): mechanism component unfilled on endpoint evidence alone

metaorthemma.reason: bound comparison protocol: which trajectory statistics count, at what resolution, over what interval, with declared claim scope

fixtures.title: new cue rebound to an existing state
fixtures.setup: A new cue is introduced and mapped onto an already existing latent state. This is observation/emission rebinding (category 1 of the five-way distinction): the latent state set is unchanged, the transition model is unchanged, the analysis version is unchanged. No new orthemic distinction is created. Provenance and version records still update. It is NOT automatically a metaorthemma: the M1 material-binding criteria are not met here, so the zero-burden rule applies.

typed_objects.orthemma: m_t: this encounter under the new cue
typed_objects.observation: x_t = Omega_A(m_t): the new cue, emitting from an existing symbol
typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t: unchanged set; no member added
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): concentrates on the same pre-existing state
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_t: shifted input coding, same downstream state occupancy
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m): unchanged at the governed level

metaorthemma.reason: zero-burden rule: a cue remapping with default scope, no case-specific binding map, no independent validity condition and no distinct binder warrant gets no token

fixtures.title: stretched/perturbed environment (inferred state is not ground truth)
fixtures.setup: The environment is perturbed relative to the conditions under which the model was fitted. The inferred latent state diverges from the situation as declared under A. The inferred state was never ground truth; the model is revised explicitly, and every claim carried across the revision is re-established or re-scoped with the carry recorded. Silent transport would be an EVIDENCE_CURRENT failure.

typed_objects.orthemma: m_t: this encounter in the perturbed environment
typed_objects.observation: x_t = Omega_A(m_t): reading produced under out-of-fit conditions
typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t: candidates from the pre-perturbation fit, now suspect
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): confidently concentrated and nonetheless wrong
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_t: activity consistent with the stale fit
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m): diverges from O*(m_t; A); recorded as incorrect, not redefined

metaorthemma.reason: bound perturbation family, revision trigger, re-establishment protocol and claim scope for the transported claims

fixtures.title: continuous-cell / discrete-population negative control
fixtures.setup: The implementing elements form a continuum of graded response profiles with plastic tuning, individual elements changing functional role over time, while the population- or model-level structure is discrete under the declared analysis. NEGATIVE CONTROL: no element is an ortheme, no one-element-one-type reading is licensed, and no localization claim is made.

typed_objects.latent_candidates: C^latent_t: discrete under A, at the population/model level only
typed_objects.belief_or_posterior: b_t(z): a distribution over that discrete set
typed_objects.internal_representation: y_t: a continuum of graded, role-mobile element responses
typed_objects.inferred_partial_profile: p-hat_t(m): a profile under A, not a property of any element

metaorthemma.reason: zero-burden rule: no material case-specific binding in the control
